proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect of ECOG performance status on progression-free survival).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to stage_iv=0.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse survival in older patients).
sex_female is associated with different pfs_months than sex_female=0.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG strongly negatively prognostic: each 1-unit higher ECOG associated with -1.190 months PFS, p~0.
Stage IV vs non-stage IV: -1.352 months PFS, p~0.
Age has POSITIVE effect on PFS (counter to hypothesis): +0.176 months/year, p~0. Older patients have BETTER PFS in this cohort.
Sex not associated with PFS: coef=-0.006, p=0.77.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0.
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.
msi_high=1, her2_amplified=1, ntrk_fusion=1, nras_mutation=1 each have a non-zero main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS mutation: -0.327 months PFS, p=8.5e-56. Strong negative prognostic.
BRAF V600E: -0.228 months PFS, p=3.7e-06. Significant negative prognostic.
Right-sided primary: -0.305 months PFS, p=5.2e-46. Strong negative prognostic.

MSI-high: diff=-0.019, p=0.68 (null). HER2 amp: -0.056, p=0.35 (null). NTRK fusion: +0.040, p=0.78 (null). NRAS mutation: +0.221, p=0.0002 (small positive in univariate, attenuates after adjusting for KRAS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Albumin: +0.465 months PFS per g/dL, p=3.3e-115. Strong positive prognostic.
CEA: -0.0053 months PFS per ng/mL, p=3.1e-17. Significant negative prognostic.
Weight loss %: -0.073 months PFS per percent, p=2.2e-167. Strong negative prognostic.
LDH: -0.0003, p=0.013 (small). CRP: -0.0020, p=0.065 (NS). NLR: +0.005, p=0.29 (NS). Alk phos: -0.0006, p=0.004 (small). Most labs apart from albumin/CEA/weight-loss are weakly or not prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months.
treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months.
treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months.
treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab on vs off: 4.285 vs 4.323, diff=-0.038, p=0.089. No significant main effect.
Bevacizumab: diff=-0.019, p=0.354. Null.
Pembrolizumab: diff=+0.005, p=0.86. Null.
Encorafenib: diff=+0.005, p=0.87. Null.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib: diff=-0.044, p=0.25. Null.
Regorafenib: 5.089 vs 4.117, diff=+0.972, p~0. STRONG positive main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0 (positive interaction).
treatment_encorafenib benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=1 than braf_v600e=0.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_amplified=1 than her2_amplified=0.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab x MSI-high interaction: coef=0.002, p=0.99. Treatment effect in MSI-high+: +0.007 (n=396 on); in MSI-high-: +0.005. NO biomarker enrichment.
Encorafenib x BRAF V600E interaction: coef=-0.141, p=0.38. Effect in BRAF V600E+: -0.129 (n=242); in BRAF V600E-: +0.013. NO benefit in BRAF V600E (in fact slight non-significant negative).
Trastuzumab/tucatinib x HER2 amp interaction: coef=0.063, p=0.78. Effect in HER2+: +0.017 (n=120); in HER2-: -0.046. NO biomarker enrichment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is reduced (or reversed) in kras_mutation=1 vs kras_mutation=0.
treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is reduced in nras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, or right_sided_primary=1.
treatment_cetuximab in the canonical cetuximab-sensitive subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab x KRAS interaction: coef=+0.020, p=0.66. Effect in KRAS+: -0.028; in KRAS-: -0.048. No detectable interaction.
Cetuximab x NRAS: coef=-0.007, p=0.96. Cetuximab x BRAF: coef=-0.026, p=0.81. Cetuximab x right-side: coef=+0.026, p=0.58. No significant interactions.
Cetuximab in RAS/BRAF wt LEFT subgroup (n=16443): unadj diff=-0.067 (p=0.13); adjusted coef=-0.074 (p=0.062). NO benefit; trend toward harm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by kras_mutation status, with larger benefit in kras_mutation=0.
treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by braf_v600e status, with larger benefit in braf_v600e=0.
treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by right_sided_primary, with larger benefit in right_sided_primary=0.
treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is differential by msi_high, her2_amplified, and ntrk_fusion.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x KRAS mutation: int_coef=-1.629, p=7.7e-226. Effect in KRAS-wt: +1.656; in KRAS-mut: +0.027. KRAS mutation almost completely abolishes regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib x BRAF V600E: int_coef=-1.183, p=6.1e-23. Effect in BRAF wt: +1.027; in BRAF V600E+: -0.156. BRAF V600E abolishes (and slightly reverses) regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib x right-sided primary: int_coef=-1.454, p=4.1e-168. Effect in left-sided: +1.480; in right-sided: +0.026. Right-sided primary almost completely abolishes regorafenib benefit.
Regorafenib x MSI-high: int_coef=-0.022, p=0.85 (null). Regorafenib x HER2: int=-0.107, p=0.46 (null). Regorafenib x NTRK: int=+0.472, p=0.20 (null). None modify regorafenib effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS wild-type left-sided) experience greater treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months than the rest of the cohort.
Adding braf_v600e=0 to the kras=0/left-sided subgroup further increases treatment_regorafenib benefit.
treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months persists across nras_mutation status when conditioning on kras_mutation=0 and right_sided_primary=0 and braf_v600e=0.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS wt + LEFT (n=18853): regorafenib on=6.639 (n=3763), off=4.119, diff=+2.520, p~0. Vs complement: very small effect.
Adding BRAF wt: subgroup n=17372, regorafenib on=6.875 (n=3461), off=4.117, diff=+2.758, p~0. Cleaner separation.

analyses.code: stratified comparisons by nras within KRAS-/BRAF-/LEFT
analyses.result_summary: NRAS mut + KRAS wt + LEFT + BRAF wt: n_on=197, diff=+2.906. NRAS wt + same: diff similar. NRAS not load-bearing for regorafenib effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is reduced as cea_ng_ml increases (negative interaction with continuous CEA).
treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is essentially zero when cea_ng_ml is in the upper half of the cohort distribution.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x CEA interaction: int_coef=-0.0253, p=8.7e-58. Each 1 ng/mL increase in CEA reduces regorafenib effect by ~0.025 months.
CEA Q1 (<=2 ng/mL): regorafenib effect +1.794. Q2 (2-4.4): +1.818. Q3 (4.4-10): +0.265. Q4 (>=10): +0.001. The treatment effect is concentrated in CEA Q1 and Q2 (i.e., CEA below ~5 ng/mL).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the regorafenib-responder background (kras=0, braf=0, right=0), the treatment effect is concentrated in patients with cea_ng_ml < 5 ng/mL (the clinical normal threshold).
In patients with cea_ng_ml >= 5 ng/mL but otherwise matching the responder profile (kras=0, braf=0, left-sided), the regorafenib treatment effect is essentially zero.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt + LEFT + CEA<5 (n=9326): regorafenib on=9.101 (n=1903), off=4.088 (n=7423), diff=+5.013 months PFS, p~0.
Subgroup KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt + LEFT + CEA>=5 (n=8046): regorafenib effect +0.005 months, p~1. The CEA threshold cleanly partitions responders from non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Identify the threshold of cea_ng_ml above which treatment_regorafenib has no benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan: CEA<3: +5.03; CEA<4: +5.04; CEA<5: +5.01; CEA<6: +4.55; CEA<8: +3.90; CEA<10: +3.62. Above CEA=5: effects approximately zero. Clean threshold near CEA=5 ng/mL (clinical upper limit of normal).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pooled three-way interaction model treatment_regorafenib * kras_mutation * right_sided_primary captures most of the heterogeneity in regorafenib effect on pfs_months.

When all four modifiers (kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml) are simultaneously included as treatment_regorafenib interactions, each retains an independent effect-modifying contribution.

analyses.result_summary: 3-way: regorafenib +2.520, regorafenib:kras -2.459 (p~0), regorafenib:right -2.449 (p=5.9e-286), 3-way int +2.348 (p=8.4e-112). KRAS and right side each independently abolish ~2.4 months of effect; redundant when both present.

analyses.code: smf.ols PFS ~ regorafenib + each int term
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib base coef +2.495; reg:kras -1.764 (p~0); reg:braf -1.760 (p~0); reg:right -1.451 (p~0); reg:cea -0.0246 (p~0). All four are independent significant negative modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect within the responder subgroup (kras=0, braf=0, right=0, cea<5) is robust to multivariable adjustment for prognostic confounders.
Adjusted for confounders within the responder subgroup, no other treatment (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab/tucatinib) has a non-null effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted for age, sex, ECOG, stage, albumin, CRP, NLR, weight loss, MSI, HER2, NRAS, and other treatments: regorafenib coef +4.978, p~0. Effect is robust.
Cetuximab: -0.044 (NS), bevacizumab: NS, pembrolizumab: NS, encorafenib: NS, trastuzumab/tucatinib: +0.011 (NS). Only regorafenib has effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, or sex_female (i.e., demographic/clinical heterogeneity).

treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, and treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib effects on pfs_months are modified by ecog_ps, stage_iv, age_years, or sex_female.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x ECOG: p=0.89; x stage_iv: p=0.15; x age_ge65: p=0.72; x sex_female: p=0.56. None of these modify the regorafenib effect on PFS.

analyses.code: exhaustive interaction screen for non-regorafenib treatments
analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib x age_ge65 marginal (-0.112, p=0.035); all other interactions null. No demographic effect modification of clinical importance for non-regorafenib drugs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect is modified by continuous laboratory features (albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, etc.) other than cea_ng_ml.
Within the regorafenib-responder subgroup, no laboratory feature meaningfully further modifies the treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Only cea_ng_ml shows a strong interaction (p=8.7e-58). Other labs: albumin (p=0.09), LDH (p=0.33), CRP (p=0.63), NLR (p=0.03 small), weight_loss (p=0.79), hemoglobin (p=0.64) — none clinically meaningful.

analyses.code: smf.ols treatment_regorafenib * each lab within responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-/BRAF-/LEFT/CEA<5: no lab feature significantly modifies treatment effect (all p>=0.07). Subgroup definition is complete with respect to laboratory features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-by-feature exhaustive scan reveals significant interactions ONLY for treatment_regorafenib (with kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml, nras_mutation).

analyses.code: exhaustive treatment*feature OLS scan over 6 trts x 27 features
analyses.result_summary: At p<0.001 only regorafenib interactions are significant: x kras_mutation (-1.629, p=7.7e-226), x right_sided_primary (-1.454, p=4.1e-168), x cea_ng_ml (-0.025, p=8.7e-58), x braf_v600e (-1.183, p=6.1e-23), x nras_mutation (+0.988, p=1.2e-11). No other treatment shows any significant interaction with any feature in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab in the canonical pembrolizumab-eligible subgroup (msi_high=1) is associated with longer pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
treatment_encorafenib in braf_v600e=1 patients is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib in her2_amplified=1 patients is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + ECOG + stage + albumin + CEA, subset MSI-high=1
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n=2513) adjusted: coef=+0.039, p=0.72. Null — no detectable benefit despite biological expectation.
Encorafenib in BRAF V600E (n=2272) adjusted: coef=-0.227, p=0.059. Trend toward HARM (not benefit), borderline non-significant. Counter to biological expectation.
Trastuzumab/tucatinib in HER2-amplified (n=1504) adjusted: coef=+0.136, p=0.48. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_bevacizumab in braf_v600e=1 patients is associated with shorter pfs_months (potential anti-targeted-therapy interaction).

analyses.code: subset BRAF V600E=1, smf.ols treatment_bevacizumab unadj and adj
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: bevacizumab in BRAF V600E shows diff=-0.237, p=0.007. Adjusted for ECOG, stage, albumin, CEA: coef=+0.003, p=0.85. Effect is fully explained by confounding (BRAF V600E + bevacizumab patients had worse baseline factors), not a true interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model with all features and all treatments simultaneously, the dominant predictors of pfs_months are age (positive), albumin (positive), ECOG, stage IV, KRAS mutation, BRAF V600E, right-sided primary, CEA, weight loss (all negative), and treatment_regorafenib (positive); other treatments are null.

analyses.code: smf.ols full multivariable model on pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Coefs: age +0.176 (p~0), albumin +0.470 (p~0), ECOG -1.171 (p~0), stage_iv -1.361 (p~0), kras -0.354 (p~0), braf -0.356 (p~0), right_side -0.287 (p~0), cea -0.005 (p~0), weight_loss -0.076 (p~0), regorafenib +0.943 (p~0). All other treatments NS (cetuximab -0.011, bev +0.003, pembro +0.0001, encor +0.006, tras +0.026).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect within the responder subgroup is independent of co-administered treatments (no synergy or antagonism).

analyses.code: stratify responder subgroup by each co-treatment status
analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-/BRAF-/LEFT/CEA<5 subgroup, regorafenib effect is similar regardless of co-treatments: with cetuximab +5.06 vs without +4.99; with bev +4.99 vs without +5.03; with pembro +5.15 vs without +4.99; with encor +5.08 vs without +5.01; with tras/tuca +4.75 vs without +5.04. No co-administration interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unexpected positive age effect on pfs_months is not an artifact of treatment selection; it persists after adjusting for ECOG, stage, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
The age effect persists within the regorafenib-responder subgroup specifically.

analyses.code: multivariable model with age + ECOG + stage + biomarkers + albumin + CEA + treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef +0.176 per year (p~0). Decade means: 30s=0.30; 40s=1.17; 50s=2.65; 60s=4.29; 70s=5.95; 80s=7.78. Strong, monotonic, persistent positive association between age and PFS even after adjustment.
Within responder subgroup: age coef +0.177 (p~0). Identical pattern; the age-PFS association is global to this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: nras_mutation has an independent effect on pfs_months beyond what is explained by kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary.
nras_mutation positively modifies treatment_regorafenib effect (in the same direction as KRAS-wt does).

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs_months ~ nras + kras + braf + right_sided_primary
analyses.result_summary: NRAS adjusted: coef=+0.061, p=0.32. NRAS has no independent prognostic effect once KRAS/BRAF/side are controlled. The univariate +0.22 was due to mutual exclusivity with KRAS (KRAS-mut patients have lower PFS).

Regorafenib x NRAS interaction: coef=+0.988, p=1.2e-11. NRAS-mut patients (who are by construction KRAS-wt) retain ~+1.93 month regorafenib benefit (n_on=313). Confirms that the relevant exclusion is KRAS, not RAS-pathway in general.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the four candidate effect-modifiers for treatment_regorafenib (kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, cea_ng_ml), each is necessary: removing any one and keeping the other three yields a partial-responder subgroup with attenuated benefit.

analyses.code: test each subgroup with one modifier dropped
analyses.result_summary: KRAS-/BRAF-/LEFT (any CEA): effect +2.76. KRAS-/BRAF-/CEA<5 (any side): effect dilutes. KRAS-/LEFT/CEA<5 (any BRAF): similar dilution. BRAF-/LEFT/CEA<5 (any KRAS): big dilution. All four modifiers are needed for the cleanest +5 month effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complement of the regorafenib responder subgroup (any of kras_mutation=1, braf_v600e=1, right_sided_primary=1, cea_ng_ml>=5) shows essentially zero treatment_regorafenib benefit on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=40674): regorafenib on=4.148 vs off=4.123, diff=+0.025, p=6.9e-05 (statistically detectable due to large n but clinically negligible). The full subgroup definition cleanly partitions a large responder population from an effectively non-responding majority.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: treatment_regorafenib is associated with substantially longer pfs_months (approximately +5 months) in patients meeting all four criteria simultaneously: kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5. Outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero. None of the other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib) shows a significant pfs_months effect in any subgroup tested, including their canonical biomarker-defined indication subgroups.

analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup (kras=0, braf=0, right=0, CEA<5): n=9326, regorafenib on (n=1903) PFS=9.10 mo vs off (n=7423) PFS=4.09 mo, diff=+5.013 mo, p~0. Complement (n=40674): diff=+0.025 mo. The subgroup definition is supported by independent significant interaction terms in a joint model: regorafenib:kras p~0, regorafenib:braf p~0, regorafenib:right p~0, regorafenib:cea p~0. None of the other treatments showed significant effect modification on any feature; their biomarker-eligible subgroups (pembro/MSI-high p=0.72, encor/BRAF p=0.06 trending negative, tras-tuca/HER2 p=0.48, cetux/RAS-wt-LEFT p=0.06 trending negative) all returned null adjusted estimates.
